compiled_state.title: As a developer, validate.sh reads commands.lint to run stack-appropriate linting
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Modify validate.sh to read the new commands.lint config key. Read new key first; fall back to legacy commands.lint_ruff/commands.lint_mypy when absent; emit [DSO WARN] and skip when both absent.

**Why**: Decouples the lint gate from Python assumptions, enabling Ruby and JS projects to run validate.sh without Python-specific output or errors.

**Scope**:
- IN: validate.sh changes; fallback chain (commands.lint → legacy keys → WARN+skip); [DSO WARN] and [DSO INFO] signals
- OUT: onboarding writes, gate-2d/2b changes, auto-format.sh changes

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, validate.sh on a project with commands.lint=bundle exec rubocop runs rubocop instead of ruff
  ← Satisfies: "validate.sh reads commands.lint; reads new key first, falls back to legacy; emits [DSO WARN] and skips when both absent"
- When this story is complete, validate.sh on a project with no commands.lint but legacy commands.lint_ruff set emits no warning and runs ruff (fallback)
  ← Satisfies: "validate.sh reads commands.lint; reads new key first, falls back to legacy"
- When this story is complete, validate.sh on a project with neither commands.lint nor legacy keys emits [DSO WARN] commands.lint not configured and exits 0
  ← Satisfies: "validate.sh emits [DSO WARN] and skips when both absent"
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing covering all three states: key present, key absent with legacy fallback, both absent (WARN+skip)

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Legacy fallback chain must not break Python-only projects on upgrade — key-absent path must fall through to legacy cleanly
- [Testing] Three-state test: (a) commands.lint present, (b) commands.lint absent + legacy key present, (c) both absent → WARN
- [Conflicting Assumptions] When BOTH commands.lint AND legacy keys (commands.lint_ruff/commands.lint_mypy) are present, commands.lint wins; legacy keys are ignored with [DSO INFO] to aid migration debugging

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
